meta._comment: Y-site IV compatibility data modeled after Trissel's Handbook on Injectable Drugs. C=Compatible, I=Incompatible, U=Unknown/Conditional. All data is for educational/demo purposes only.
meta.source: Trissel's 2 IV Compatibility (adapted for clinical decision support demo)

classificationKey.C: Compatible — drugs may be co-administered via Y-site at tested concentrations
classificationKey.I: Incompatible — visible precipitation, color change, or chemical degradation observed
classificationKey.U: Conditional/Unknown — compatibility depends on specific conditions noted; exercise caution

pairs.notes: Physically compatible for 4 hours at 22°C under fluorescent light.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1999;56:1456

pairs.notes: Immediate white turbidity and precipitate formation observed.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 2000;57:1120

pairs.notes: No visual or chemical changes over 4 hours at 25°C.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible at lower concentrations only. At higher concentrations (>20 mg/mL cefazolin), haze may develop after 2 hours. Monitor line visually.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1999;56:515

pairs.notes: No physical or chemical incompatibility for 24 hours at room temperature.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: White precipitate forms immediately upon mixing. Do NOT co-administer via Y-site.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1993;50:2527

pairs.notes: Physically compatible for 24 hours at 21°C. Common bedside co-infusion.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible for 4 hours at room temperature.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Immediate precipitation. Furosemide's high pH (8-9.3) is incompatible with midazolam's low pH (3).
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1995;52:2813

pairs.notes: Standard DKA co-infusion. Compatible for 24 hours.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Common antiemetic combination. Compatible for 4 hours at 22°C.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: White precipitate forms within 15 minutes. pH incompatibility.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1997;54:1766

pairs.notes: Heavy white precipitate forms immediately. Flush line between drugs. This is one of the most commonly reported Y-site incompatibilities in clinical practice.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1994;51:2599
ISMP Alert 2009

pairs.notes: Common procedural sedation combination. Compatible for 4 hours.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Phenytoin precipitates in nearly all IV solutions and with most Y-site drugs due to its extreme alkalinity (pH ~12). Administer via dedicated line only.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Immediate crystallization. Phenytoin requires dedicated line.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Immediate turbidity. Diazepam is formulated in propylene glycol and is incompatible with most aqueous IV drugs.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Commonly co-administered for electrolyte replacement. Compatible for 24 hours.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Color change observed within 1 hour. Pantoprazole has limited Y-site compatibility due to its high pH (9-10).
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 2004;61:1478

pairs.notes: Compatible for 4 hours at 22°C under fluorescent light.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Reports conflict. Some studies show compatibility at low heparin concentrations (<100 units/mL) in D5W only. Incompatible in NS. Verify diluent before Y-site administration.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 2001;58:1174
Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Immediate turbidity and precipitate formation.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1997;54:1766

pairs.notes: Compatible for 24 hours at room temperature.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible for 4 hours at 22°C.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Variable reports. Compatible in some studies at low concentrations in NS but precipitate reported in D5W. Flush line thoroughly between drugs if Y-site unavoidable.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 2003;60:1718

pairs.notes: Physically compatible for 24 hours at 22°C. Commonly co-administered in neutropenic fever protocols.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible for 24 hours at room temperature.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Physically compatible for 4 hours at 22°C.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Turbidity and precipitate observed. Do not co-administer via Y-site at these concentrations.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1999;56:1458

pairs.notes: Compatible for 24 hours at room temperature. Common ICU co-infusion.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Physically compatible. Frequently co-administered in vasopressor management.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Precipitate forms. Amiodarone should be administered through a dedicated line where possible.
references: Trissel LA. AJHP. 1998;55:1349

pairs.notes: Limited data. Amiodarone is formulated with polysorbate 80 and benzyl alcohol which may interact with other drugs. Monitor visually.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible for 4 hours at room temperature.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Both formulated in propylene glycol; compatibility data limited. Use with caution and monitor for precipitate.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Physically compatible for 4 hours at 22°C.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Compatible for 24 hours at room temperature. Common cardiac ICU combination.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.

pairs.notes: Precipitate forms immediately due to pH incompatibility.
references: Trissel LA. Handbook on Injectable Drugs, 15th ed.
